title: JSON extension accepts invalid numeric values
description:
drh added on 2017-04-08 17:54:19:
The JSON spec says that numeric
values may not have leading zeros.  The value can consist of a single zero
or a zero before the decimal point, but otherwise the first digit must be
between 1 and 9.  The SQLite JSON extension does not enforce this and allows
numeric values with an unlimited number of leading zeros.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE json_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, json_data JSON NOT NULL);
SELECT json_data FROM json_test WHERE json_extract(json_data, '$.value') = '123';
DELETE FROM json_test WHERE json_extract(json_data, '$.value') IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Allows numeric values with leading zeros in JSON
